title: Leviticus: Clean and Unclean
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of leviticus: clean and unclean and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study leviticus: clean and unclean and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What overarching principle governed Israel's clean and unclean distinctions in Leviticus 11-15? Answer: holiness — God's own holy character required Israel to be set apart from impurity as a people belonging to him.
segments.correct_answer: holiness — be holy as I am holy; clean and unclean mark Israel's separation unto God
acceptable_variants: the principle of holiness separating Israel from defilement
god is holy so his people must be pure
separation for holiness — god's people are set apart from all impurity

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The categories in Leviticus 11-15 governing food, skin conditions, and bodily discharges; they marked Israel's sacred status and regulated access to the tabernacle
The section of Leviticus (chapters 17-26) containing repeated calls to holiness, covering sacrifices, sexual ethics, feasts, and penalties for violations
